title: Finding the range
problem: Find the range of the data set $4, 9, 2, 15, 7$.

steps.instruction: Identify the highest value in the data set.
Identify the lowest value in the data set.
Subtract the lowest value from the highest value.

title: Finding the interquartile range
problem: Find the interquartile range (IQR) of the data set $3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18$.

steps.instruction: Confirm the data is sorted, then split it into a lower half and an upper half.
Find Q1, the median of the lower half.
steps.working: The median of $3, 6, 9$ is $6$, so Q1 $= 6$.

steps.instruction: Find Q3, the median of the upper half.
steps.working: The median of $12, 15, 18$ is $15$, so Q3 $= 15$.

title: Comparing the spread of two data sets
problem: Compare the spread of Set A: $\{10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20\}$ and Set B: $\{2, 8, 14, 20, 26, 32\}$ using both range and IQR.

steps.instruction: Find the range of each set.
steps.working: Set A: range $= 20 - 10 = 10$. Set B: range $= 32 - 2 = 30$.

steps.instruction: Find Q1 and Q3 for Set A.
Find Q1 and Q3 for Set B.
Compare the measures and draw a conclusion.
steps.working: Set B has a greater range ($30 > 10$) and a greater IQR ($18 > 6$), so Set B is more spread out by both measures.

answer: Set B is more spread out, with a greater range ($30$ vs $10$) and a greater IQR ($18$ vs $6$).
